interactions.summary: Kim asks whether Jiang can teach this material without trouble and probes the legal-school category. Jiang answers that he operates in a gray area between public schools and expat schools: private experimental schools for Chinese passport holders are tolerated if they avoid Communist Party red lines.

speaker_notes.note: A boundary repair leaves some turns awkward around seg-0027 to seg-0029, but the exchange remains Kim probing school categories and Jiang distinguishing private experimental Chinese schools from expat-track international schools.

claims.claim: Jiang says public schools in China are heavily monitored through textbooks and tests, producing extreme conformity in the education system.
Private international or experimental schools form a gray area because they serve a small minority of students going abroad, and the government has not fully settled how to regulate them.
claims.temporal_scope: Education-system diagnosis on 2026-04-01, describing a recent roughly 10-year phenomenon.

claims.claim: Jiang says he can teach unusually blunt material in China only because he works in this murky gray area and avoids Communist Party red lines.
The school category is distinct from expat-only international schools requiring foreign passports; Jiang says his students are Chinese passport holders in an in-between experimental category.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang locates his freedom not in formal liberty but in an administrative gray zone too odd to fully police.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I operate in a very gray area in China
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains the paradox that this material appears from Beijing while Jiang says it would be impossible elsewhere.

signature_moments.moment: The more off-the-wall the class sounds, the more it can hide in plain sight.
signature_moments.source_phrase: so wacky that most people... tune off
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This gives the public read a sharp mechanism for how taboo speech survives through disbelief.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: The regulatory space between Chinese public schools and expat-only international schools where Jiang says experimental private education can occur.
Topics or actions that offend the Communist Party and therefore cannot be crossed in Jiang's teaching environment.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang describes the experimental Chinese-passport-holder international-school category as a recent phenomenon of roughly the past 10 years as of 2026-04-01.

uncertainty_notes.note: The transcript appears to blend or mis-bound speaker turns around 'that's the school that I'm at'; the semantic meaning is treated cautiously.
